title: Cell growth and harvest for E. coli-based Cell-free Protein Synthesis
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"6k9rf","text":"Cell-free protein synthesis reactions require cell-free extract derived from rapidly dividing E. coli cells to produce high yields of protein therapeutics, antigens, enzymes, vaccines, and other proteins. This protocol describes the cell growth and harvest steps for E. coli to produce extract.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"begbf","text":"","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"fsmle","text":"Read more on cell-free protein synthesis here: https://www.swiftscalebio.com/blog/cell-free-protein-synthesis ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[{"key":0,"offset":47,"length":62}],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{"0":{"type":"link","mutability":"","data":{"guid":"C99B5D32C7D811EBAFB60A58A9FEAC02","url":"https://www.swiftscalebio.com/blog/cell-free-protein-synthesis"}}}}

steps: Start a 50 mL seed culture of E. coli strain and grow in LB media Overnight at 37 °C .
Add 250 mL glucose to 750 mL 2xYTP media in Tunair flask to obtain 2xYTPG media.
Inoculate Tunair flask culture at approximately OD 600 = 0.05.
Grow culture at 37 °C with shaking at 250 rpm, °C, to OD 600 = 0.6 and induce T7 with IPTG (if necessary). Add to 1 millimolar (mM) IPTG.
Harvest culture at OD 600 = 3 by centrifugation at 8000 x g, °C, for 00:05:00 and washing with S30 buffer three times.

After the last wash, pour off S30 supernatant to obtain the pellet. Resuspend this pellet in S30 and lyse or immediately flash freeze on liquid nitrogen and store at -80 °C until ready for cell lysis.
creator: Emily Pennell
